subject: Ecology
title: Pesticides and Pollinators
description: A quick, practical guide to Pesticides and Pollinators.

hook.text: Neonicotinoid pesticides don't always kill bees outright—they scramble their navigation.

buildup.text: Neonics are systemic—absorbed into every part of the plant including pollen and nectar.

discovery.text: Exposed bees lose the ability to find their way home, forage efficiently, or fight off diseases.

twist.text: Sublethal doses accumulate over time, weakening entire colonies without a single dramatic kill.

climax.text: The EU banned outdoor use of three neonics in 2018 after studies confirmed harm to pollinators.

punchline.text: The poison doesn't kill instantly. It confuses to death.

quiz.question: How do neonicotinoids harm bees at sublethal doses?
options: They impair navigation, foraging, and disease resistance
